question: What techniques exist for self-improving agents without fine-tuning?

heuristic_evaluation.question: What techniques exist for self-improving agents without fine-tuning?

coverage.aspect: limits of self-improvement without external feedback

claim_lines: Self-improving agents without fine-tuning usually use **test-time loops** instead of weight updates: (1) **verbal feedback + reflection** stored in memory, (2) **self-refinement** where the same model critiques and revises its own output, and (3) **prompt/workflow optimization** where candidate prompts or solutions are evaluated and fed into the next iteration. These methods can improve performance without updating model weights. ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366))

- **verbal feedback** — covered by Reflexion’s use of task feedback signals, including scalar or free-form feedback, turned into verbal reflections. [Reflexion](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366) ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366))

- **reflection** — covered by Reflexion’s explicit “verbally reflect” step and reuse of that text in later trials. [Reflexion](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366) ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.11366))

- **self-refinement** — covered by Self-Refine’s same-model generate/feedback/refine loop with no extra training or RL. [Self-Refine](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.17651) ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2303.17651))

- **prompt or workflow optimization** — covered by OPRO, which iteratively optimizes prompts/solutions using evaluated candidates. [OPRO](https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.03409) ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2309.03409))

- **limits of self-improvement without external feedback** — covered by evidence that intrinsic self-correction can fail or degrade without external feedback, though another paper reports success under zero temperature and unbiased prompts. [Cannot Self-Correct](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.01798), [Intrinsic Self-Correction Ability](https://arxiv.org/pdf/2406.15673.pdf) ([arxiv.org](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.01798))

coverage_audit.reason: Explicitly identifies verbal feedback in Reflexion and ties it to reflections/memory.
Directly explains reflection as a verbal self-critique step reused in later attempts.
Correctly describes Self-Refine as an iterative generate/feedback/refine loop without training.
Includes prompt/workflow optimization via OPRO-style iterative evaluation and prompt improvement.

coverage_audit.aspect: limits of self-improvement without external feedback
coverage_audit.reason: Mentions that self-correction can fail or degrade without external feedback and notes some contradictory findings, though the limitations are not deeply unpacked.

citation_audit.claim: Self-improving agents without fine-tuning usually use test-time loops instead of weight updates, including verbal feedback/reflection, self-refinement, and prompt/workflow optimization.
citation_audit.reason: This is a reasonable high-level synthesis consistent with the cited Reflexion paper and the other cited papers named in the answer.

citation_audit.claim: Reflexion uses task feedback signals, including scalar or free-form feedback, turned into verbal reflections.
citation_audit.reason: Plausibly supported by the Reflexion paper title and known topic; the claim is directly aligned with the paper.

citation_audit.claim: Self-Refine uses a same-model generate/feedback/refine loop with no extra training or RL.
citation_audit.reason: Directly matches the Self-Refine paper's subject and stated mechanism.

citation_audit.claim: OPRO iteratively optimizes prompts/solutions using evaluated candidates.
citation_audit.reason: This is the core topic of the cited 'Large Language Models as Optimizers' paper.

citation_audit.claim: Intrinsic self-correction can fail or degrade without external feedback, though another paper reports success under zero temperature and unbiased prompts.
citation_audit.reason: The cited papers are directly about self-correction limitations and potential intrinsic self-correction ability.

source_audit.reason: Primary source and directly relevant Reflexion paper.
Primary source and directly relevant Self-Refine paper.
Primary source for prompt optimization / LLM-as-optimizer ideas.
Primary source for limits of self-correction without external feedback.
Relevant follow-up paper on OPRO limitations, though not essential to the core answer.
Relevant academic source, but the PDF URL is less clean than an abs page and the exact claim is more nuanced.
Likely duplicate/format variant of the same paper; still relevant but redundant.
Secondary site rather than primary literature; not needed given the arXiv source.
Low-authority discussion thread; not appropriate as evidence for this benchmark answer.

judge_evaluation.summary: A strong, mostly complete answer with the right techniques and relevant primary sources. Main weaknesses are light treatment of the limits/trade-offs and some unnecessary low-quality source inclusion.
judge_evaluation.recommended_fix: Deepen the limitations section with concrete failure modes and remove secondary/low-authority citations in favor of only primary papers.
